channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Un personaje infantil desató una batalla cultural en Rusia
item.description: Influyentes conservadores rusos sostienen que, en lugar de obsesionarse con el personaje ficticio Cheburashka, el país debería concentrarse en asuntos más importantes, como el renacimiento de un imperio ruso.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Nazaryan

item.title: Una guerra para un hombre
item.description: El primer ministro de Israel no necesita una victoria limpia, solo requiere una narrativa duradera.
item.dc:creator: Mairav Zonszein
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: As Income Gap Grows, So Do Fears Over Access to a Quality Education
item.description: Leaders and parents worry that a widening economic divide amid the current affordability crisis could amplify the role that money plays in access to a robust education in New York.
item.dc:creator: Troy Closson

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: At Cristo Rey Brooklyn High School in New York, tuition is manageable for families, who pay about $1,700 a year on average.

item.title: Picture Prompt: Hollywood’s Big Night
item.description: What was the best movie you saw in the past year?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Michael B. Jordan with is Oscar for best actor.

item.title: A Red Fox’s Fantastic Voyage Starts in England and Leads to the Bronx
item.description: The fox is convalescing at the Bronx Zoo after an unlikely 3,600-mile sea crossing from England to the Port of New York and New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan
item.category: Port of New York and New Jersey

media:content.media:description: A red fox found aboard a cargo ship from England last month was taken to the Bronx Zoo, where he is served a varied diet of fruits, vegetables, insects and mice.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 17, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: In Rural New York, Some See Proposed A.I. Center as a Needless Intrusion
item.description: The data center, to be built between Buffalo and Rochester, will raise electric bills and harm a nearby tribal reservation, opponents argue. “I can’t think of one good reason for it,” a local resident said.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sommer

media:content.media:credit: Lauren Petracca for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The center would be built at an industrial park in Alabama, N.Y. Its developer, the Dallas-based Stream Data Centers, would receive $1.4 billion in tax subsidies.

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Has a Bone to Pick With Trump’s War Plans
item.description: “The only war Trump had an exit plan for was Vietnam,” Kimmel remarked after the president said he would end the war in Iran when he “feels it in his bones.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Word of the Day: adverse
item.description: This word has appeared in 391 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: What the Iran War Means for the U.S.-China Relationship
item.description: With the Xi-Trump summit almost certainly delayed, and tensions rising over the war in Iran, vital issues for both the U.S. and China are also being cast into uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The Chinese destroyer Tangshan at dock near an Iranian navy ship in Cape Town, South Africa, in January for joint maritime safety operations.

item.title: Un turista alemán exigió 20 millones de dólares tras un viaje fallido a NY
item.description: Faycal Manz, turista alemán, alegó que un taco le causó diarrea, que un Walmart lo discriminó y que la policía le provocó insomnio.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag

media:content.media:description: La salsa de Los Tacos N.o 1, una franquicia de restaurantes de Manhattan, fue objeto de una de las tres demandas presentadas por Faycal Manz tras un breve viaje a Estados Unidos en 2024.

item.title: Trump’s Dangerous Lack of a Strategy in Iran
item.description: The early reality of the Iran war is not cooperating with President Trump’s bluster.
item.dc:creator: The Editorial Board
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Rebecca Chew/The New York Times

item.title: Ellas mataron a sus agresores. ¿Deben pasar el resto de su vida en la cárcel?
item.description: Se suponía que una nueva ley ayudaría a reducir las condenas de las sobrevivientes de la violencia doméstica, pero la mayoría sigue tras las rejas.
item.dc:creator: Pamela Colloff

media:content.media:credit: Carolyn Drake para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: April Wilkens en el Centro Correccional Mabel Bassett de McLoud, Oklahoma, en agosto.

item.title: Harry Styles lanza su cuarto álbum. Ahora tiene competencia
item.description: En los cuatro años desde la última vez que el cantante británico presentó un disco, otros artistas masculinos con estéticas similares han escalado en las listas de éxitos.
item.dc:creator: Shaad D’Souza
item.category: Kiss All the Time. Disco Occasionally. (Album)

item.title: El gobierno de Trump le habría dicho a Cuba que debe cambiar de presidente
item.description: EE. UU. le ha dicho a Cuba que, para que se produzcan avances significativos en las negociaciones, el presidente Miguel Díaz-Canel debe dimitir, dijeron personas familiarizadas con las conversaciones.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles, Edward Wong and Annie Correal

media:content.media:description: El presidente de Cuba, Miguel Díaz-Canel, durante una visita a Venezuela el año pasado.

item.title: Inside the Katseye Fandom: Friendships, Rivalries and Boycotts Over Manon’s Hiatus
item.description: Fans of the group have leveraged their influence into friendship and rivalry. Then a member’s sudden hiatus sent them into a spiral.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai and Matt Yan

media:content.media:credit: Alexander Coggin for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “You have to remind yourself that you don’t know them personally,” said Joy Lebofsky, who has experienced the joys and disappointments of being a stan.

item.title: As American Bombers Take Off From R.A.F. Fairford in UK, Iraq War Looms Large
item.description: R.A.F. Fairford was the site of repeated antiwar protests during the Iraq war in 2003. Now it is being used again as a base for U.S. bombing missions in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: U.S. Air Force personnel loading munitions into a B-1 bomber at R.A.F. Fairford on Monday.

item.title: As War With Iran Hurts Oil Prices, U.S. Turns to Iranian Boats for Help
item.description: Iranian-linked ships carrying Russian oil were among those that received temporary exemptions from sanctions, a sign of how dire the energy crisis is becoming.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Krolik
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A tanker in the Persian Gulf last week. Ships carrying Russian oil as of Thursday are temporarily exempt from U.S. sanctions.

item.title: Corrections: March 17, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, March 17, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Trump Faults Allies Over Their Lukewarm Responses to Securing Strait
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, March 17, 2026.

item.title: Oil Begins Flowing Through California Pipeline Under Trump Order
item.description: Oil had not flowed through the pipeline since a 2015 rupture caused an environmental disaster on California’s Central Coast. It sets up a new fight between the Trump administration and state officials.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla

media:content.media:description: A leaky pipeline was the source of a spill, seen in this 2015 file photo, that polluted Refugio State Beach, north of Goleta, Calif., and killed hundreds of birds and marine mammals. The pipeline’s new owner, Sable Offshore, says the problems have been fixed and that it has restarted the flow of oil at the direction of the Trump administration.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Monday
item.description: President Trump said he might postpone a trip to China to focus on the war. More than a million people have been displaced in Lebanon this month, according to the country’s health ministry.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: An encampment in a parking lot in Beirut, Lebanon. Israel’s defense minister, Israel Katz, said that its forces had launched a “ground maneuver” in southern Lebanon, compounding fears of a broader invasion.

item.title: Cannonball with Wesley Morris: What the Oscars Got Right
item.description: Giving Michael B. Jordan and “KPop Demon Hunters” their due, to start.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Morris and Sasha Weiss

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; Photo: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 17, 2026
item.description: Kiran Pandey presents a puzzle for our modern times.
item.dc:creator: Amy Virshup

media:content.media:description: Dance, solve, repeat.

item.title: University of Florida Penalizes GOP Group Over Antisemitism Accusations
item.description: After the University of Florida restricted the Republican organization from operating on campus, the group sued the university arguing its First Amendment rights were violated.
item.dc:creator: Dana Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: Jacob Langston for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The University of Florida’s interim president, Donald W. Landry, said the school supports its Jewish students.

item.title: How Michael B. Jordan Won (and Timothée Chalamet Lost) Best Actor at 2026 Oscars
item.description: The “Sinners” star became the consensus pick as voters soured on Timothée Chalamet, the “Marty Supreme” lead. It helped that the winner is a bankable performer.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Michael B. Jordan with his statuette on Sunday night.

item.title: Kouri Richins Found Guilty of Poisoning Her Husband in Utah Murder Trial
item.description: Kouri Richins was accused of mixing a lethal dose of fentanyl into a drink she made for her husband. She later wrote a children’s book about coping with grief.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin

media:content.media:description: Kouri Richins, a Utah mother accused of fatally poisoning her husband, during her murder trial at the Summit County Courthouse in Park City, Utah.

item.title: Richard Grenell: From Trump Cabinet Hopeful to Ex-Kennedy Center Manager
item.description: Richard Grenell once hoped to be President Trump’s secretary of state. Instead, Mr. Trump just replaced him as Kennedy Center president.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Williamson
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:description: Richard Grenell, left, the outgoing president of the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts, was seated next to his replacement, Matt Floca, the center’s vice president of facilities operations, at a White House news conference on Monday.

item.title: The Shortsightedness of Trump’s War in Iran
item.description: Is the U.S. repeating a century-old mistake in Iran? On “The Ezra Klein Show,” Ali Vaez, the Iran project director for the International Crisis Group, warns that Western-backed “short-term gains” often lead to unintended consequences.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Trump Claims an Ex-President Confided His Regrets on Iran. But Who?
item.description: The New York Times reached out to people close to President Trump’s predecessors. They disputed Mr. Trump’s claims.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Monday.

item.title: Trump’s Failed Diplomacy in Iran
item.description: What is the price of President Trump’s failed diplomacy? Ali Vaez, the Iran project director at the International Crisis Group, tells Ezra Klein that Trump’s preference for “surrender” over “marginal improvements” cost the U.S. a historic nuclear breakthrough.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Airport Security Lines Grow Amid Partial Government Shutdown
item.description: Travelers face long lines at airport security checkpoints at a growing number of U.S. airports as Congress remains at an impasse over funding for the Department of Homeland Security, which oversees the T.S.A.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: How Trump Destroyed the Aura of the Wartime President
item.description: Even as he’s wrecking American institutions, he is revealing the limits of his cultural influence.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Fast-Moving Fire in Queens Kills 4, Including a Child
item.description: Fierce winds fueled a blaze in a mixed-use building on Monday, killing four people and injuring 12 others, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal and Jackie Molloy

media:content.media:credit: Jackie Molloy for The New York Times

item.title: Aide Accused by Hegseth of Leaking Is Hired for Intelligence Job
item.description: Dan Caldwell will serve as an adviser to senior intelligence officials. His hiring signals the end of a high-profile investigation.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Office of the Director of National Intelligence

media:content.media:credit: Justin T. Gellerson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An investigation turned up no proof that Dan Caldwell or two other Trump administration officials committed any wrongdoing.

item.title: Administration Targeted Climate Lab Over Tina Peters, Lawsuit Says
item.description: The complaint says efforts to dismantle an atmospheric research center are part of a broader political campaign that endangers climate and weather studies.
item.dc:creator: Eric Niiler

media:content.media:description: The National Center for Atmospheric Research in Boulder, Colo.

item.title: Gregory Bovino to Retire From Border Patrol
item.description: The outspoken border official drove the agency’s aggressive immigration policy in U.S. cities, including Minneapolis, until January.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:description: Gregory Bovino had a decades-long career with the U.S. Border Patrol. His brash style drew criticism from both the left and the right.

item.title: Trump Administration Seeks to Remove Cuba’s President From Power During Negotiations
item.description: The United States has told Cuba that for meaningful progress to be made in negotiations, President Miguel Díaz-Canel must step down, said people familiar with the talks.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles, Edward Wong and Annie Correal

media:content.media:description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel of Cuba during a visit to Venezuela last year.

item.title: House Adopts Bill to Ease Recovery by Heirs of Nazi Looted Art
item.description: The Senate had already passed an extension of the so-called HEAR Act, which is scheduled to expire at the end of the year. The bill now heads to President Trump’s desk.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Hickley and Zachary Small
item.category: SNCF (Societe Nationale des Chemins de fer Francais)

media:content.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The bill would extend what is known as the Holocaust Expropriated Art Recovery Act of 2016, which was designed to address cases where museums had blocked claims by invoking the statute of limitations.

item.title: A Judge Strikes Down Kennedy’s Vaccine Policies
item.description: Also, Trump raises the possibility of the U.S. “taking Cuba.” Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

item.title: Trump Is Trying to Sell War Like Steaks
item.description: The president is approaching the war in Iran as if he’s selling another Trump product, the writer E.J. Dionne Jr. says on “The Opinions.” But “selling war is a very serious business,” he adds, unlike selling Trump Steaks or Trump University.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Why Affordability Still Hurts Democrats
item.description: Affordability is Democrats’ new buzzword, the Republican pollster Kristen Soltis Anderson says. But what’s their plan to actually lower prices? She explains on “The Opinions.”

item.title: Trump dice que tendrá el ‘honor’ de ‘tomar Cuba’
item.description: Las palabras del presidente Trump se produjeron cuando se anticipaba que las autoridades de Cuba anunciaran una medida para abrir la economía a los inversores extranjeros, entre ellos los exiliados cubanos.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal, Jack Nicas and Frances Robles

media:content.media:description: El barrio de Güinera de La Habana el viernes.

item.title: How ‘Marty Supreme’ and Timothée Chalamet Went Home With No Oscars
item.description: A few years ago, the indie studio A24 was luxuriating in Academy Awards. On Sunday it was shut out entirely.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Sperling

media:content.media:description: “Marty Supreme,” directed by Josh Safdie and starring Timothée Chalamet, received nine Oscar nominations and won none.

item.title: ICE Releases Columbia Protester Who Was Held for One Year
item.description: Leqaa Kordia, 33, had been held in a Texas facility, where she said her health had declined. She was arrested during a 2024 protest and then detained by ICE in March 2025.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cramer

media:content.media:description: Leqaa Kordia was released on Monday after a year in federal immigration detention. She has not been charged with a crime.

item.title: Inside the Democrats’ Infighting in the Illinois Primary
item.description: We talked to our Peoria-born reporter about Primary Day in what he claims is America’s best state.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:description: From left, Representative Robin Kelly, Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi and Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton are running in the Democratic primary for Senate in Illinois.

item.title: War Isn’t the Only Thing on Trump’s Mind
item.description: President Trump spoke to journalists for about 40 minutes before holding a news conference on Monday, offering a glimpse into his priorities.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:description: President Trump on Monday at the White House, where he went on a series of tangents that were notable even for a president who boasts about his oratorical “weave.”

item.title: A Predictable Problem
item.description: Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a well-known problem, appears to have caught Trump off guard.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.S. Court Rules Against RFK Jr.’s Vaccine Policies
item.description: The decision by Judge Brian Murphy immediately halted implementation of a series of measures imposed by the Trump administration that were aimed at upending decades of immunization standards.

item.title: Fire on the U.S. Aircraft Carrier Gerald R. Ford Raged for Hours, Sailors Say
item.description: The Ford is now entering its 10th month of deployment after arriving in the Middle East from the Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The crew on the U.S.S. Gerald R. Ford have been told that their deployment will likely be extended into May, which would put them at an entire year at sea.
